extra.reasoning: abdominal_pain appears in exactly one rule, r3 (priority 100, the highest priority in the system), whose consequent is the LOWEST acuity (self_care). r3 fires only when all of {focal_weakness, vomiting, confusion, stiff_neck, dyspnea, back_pain, vision_loss, pallor, palpitations, abdominal_pain} are true. Consider the witness input where those first 9 conjuncts are all true and fever=false. With abdominal_pain=false, r3 does not fire; the firing rules include r5/r6/r8/r9 at priority 3 (emergent), and no rule has higher priority among the firing ones, so acuity=emergent. With abdominal_pain=true (everything else unchanged), all 10 conjuncts of r3 are satisfied, so r3 fires at priority 100, overriding all priority-3 emergent rules, giving acuity=self_care. Thus flipping abdominal_pain from false to true drops acuity from emergent (highest) to self_care (lowest), violating the monotonicity property that setting abdominal_pain true must never lower acuity. The witness_assignment is the abdominal_pain=true state; its counterpart with abdominal_pain=false yields the higher acuity (emergent).
